meta.notes: Heuristic rules for static/LLM-assisted review. Confirm findings against framework version and project conventions.

error.label: High risk or incorrect framework use

scoring.normalisation: After summing penalties for matched rules in one review unit, score = max(0, min(10, initial_score - min(sum_penalties, max_total_penalty_per_file))).

interpretation.9_to_10: Senior-quality for the reviewed surface: idiomatic Laravel, safe boundaries, testable seams.
interpretation.7_to_8: Acceptable with minor issues: tighten validation, policies, or query shape.
interpretation.5_to_6: Risky merge: likely bugs, auth gaps, or performance traps.
interpretation.0_to_4: Do not merge without rework: security, correctness, or ops risk.

rules.title: Bloated controller action
rules.description: Controllers should orchestrate HTTP concerns; heavy domain logic belongs in actions, services, or domain classes.

heuristics: Multiple database writes in one action

examples.junior_or_risky: public function store(Request $request) { /* 200 lines: validate inline, charge card, send mail, log analytics */ }

rules.title: Mass assignment from all input
rules.description: Using $request->all() or unvalidated arrays for fillable updates is a common source of privilege escalation and data corruption.

rules.title: Validation only in controller
rules.description: Inline $request->validate([...]) everywhere duplicates rules and blocks reuse from jobs/commands.

rules.title: Authorisation missing on mutation
rules.description: Destructive routes should be gated by policies or explicit authorisation checks.

heuristics: Route uses delete/put/patch without Authorize middleware or $this->authorize

rules.title: Policy logic duplicated across controllers
rules.description: Repeated role checks in controllers instead of a policy method increases drift.

rules.title: N+1 query risk in loops
rules.description: Accessing lazy-loaded relations inside a loop causes N+1 queries.

rules.title: Unbounded query
rules.description: Missing pagination or limits on user-driven lists risks memory and timeouts.

rules.title: Raw SQL without bindings
rules.description: DB::raw / whereRaw with string concatenation invites SQL injection.

rules.title: Non-idempotent job side effects
rules.description: Jobs that double-charge, double-email, or duplicate rows on retry need idempotency keys or unique constraints.

heuristics: Dispatches external payment without idempotency reference
Inserts without unique index on natural key

examples.senior: Use ShouldBeUnique / unique DB constraint / idempotency token stored before external call.

rules.title: Heavy work in sync request path
rules.description: Long I/O in HTTP requests hurts UX; push to queue with clear failure handling.

rules.title: No failure handling for queueable
rules.description: Jobs should define $tries/backoff or failed() and log context; silent loss is common.

examples.junior_or_risky: class SendAlert implements ShouldQueue { public function handle() { /* no failed() */ } }

rules.title: Over-chunked fan-out without rate control
rules.description: Spawning huge numbers of jobs without batching or throttling can overwhelm workers and downstream APIs.

rules.title: Multi-step money movement without transaction
rules.description: Ledger updates spanning multiple writes should be atomic to avoid partial commits.

heuristics: Multiple balance updates in one method

rules.title: Long transaction holding locks
rules.description: External HTTP or slow queries inside DB::transaction increases deadlock risk.

examples.senior: Reserve rows in a short transaction; perform I/O after commit.

rules.title: Implicit lazy loading in API resource
rules.description: API resources accessing relations without eager loading cause N+1 in production.

pair_with_controller: Controller missing with() for same relation names

examples.senior: Controller: Model::with('user'); // or Resource::collection with eager loads

rules.title: Tenant scope bypass via unscoped query
rules.description: Queries without tenant constraint can leak data across organisations.

heuristics: Project uses tenant packages or tenant_id columns
FirstOrFail on global model without tenant filter

examples.senior: Document::where('tenant_id', tenant('id'))->findOrFail($id); // or global scope trait

rules.title: Tenant identifier taken only from request body
rules.description: Trusting client-supplied tenant_id is unsafe; derive from authenticated context.

examples.senior: Resolve tenant from subdomain / authenticated membership, not raw input.

rules.title: Webhook handler not idempotent
rules.description: Payment and provider webhooks retry; handlers must dedupe by event id or signature + stored state.

heuristics: Creates invoice without checking processed event id

rules.title: Explicit unique job constraint
rules.description: ShouldBeUnique or WithoutOverlapping reduces duplicate side effects under load.

examples.junior_or_risky: class Sync implements ShouldQueue { /* no uniqueness */ }
examples.senior: class Sync implements ShouldQueue, ShouldBeUnique { public function uniqueId() { ... } }

rules.title: Filament table query without tenant scoping
rules.description: Admin tables must respect tenant/global scopes; unscoped Eloquent in resources leaks rows.

examples.junior_or_risky: public static function getEloquentQuery(): Builder { return parent::getEloquentQuery()->withoutGlobalScopes(); }
examples.senior: Apply tenant scope in getEloquentQuery or use policies + model scopes deliberately.

rules.title: Heavy logic in Filament form callbacks
rules.description: afterStateUpdated with large side effects is hard to test; push to actions/services.

examples.senior: Dispatch job or call application service with validated state.

rules.title: Feature test without auth context for protected route
rules.description: Happy-path tests hitting secured routes without actingAs or assertForbidden miss authorisation regressions.

rules.title: Database tests without RefreshDatabase or proper isolation
rules.description: Flaky tests often come from shared DB state; use RefreshDatabase or transactions per test strategy.

heuristics: No RefreshDatabase in Pest uses() or PHPUnit

rules.title: Over-mocked HTTP without asserting outbound contract
rules.description: Http::fake without asserting request shape can hide breaking API changes.

rules.title: Excessive abstraction for trivial CRUD
rules.description: Many indirection layers for simple resources slows onboarding without adding safety.

examples.junior_or_risky: Controller -> Manager -> Service -> Repository -> Model for one update
examples.senior: Action + model for small apps; repository when persistence complexity warrants it.

rules.title: Explicit eager loads at boundary
rules.description: Controllers or query objects calling with() for all rendered relations is a strong N+1 mitigation signal.
